data.title: Write failing tests for scaffold template output correctness (Flask blueprint, NextJS page/API)
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Create tests/scripts/test-scaffold-templates.sh — tests verifying that scaffold-adapter.sh generates correct, syntactically valid boilerplate content. Tests must fail (RED) before templates and adapter exist.

### Tests:
1. test_flask_blueprint_syntax: Run scaffold-route with flask/users. Generated blueprint.py is valid Python (python3 -m py_compile).
2. test_flask_view_function_present: Generated file contains a view function decorated with @route or blueprint.route.
3. test_flask_template_html_valid: Generated template.html is well-formed HTML (contains <html>, <body> tags or extends base).
4. test_nextjs_page_valid_js: Generated page file is valid JavaScript/TypeScript (node --check or similar).
5. test_nextjs_api_route_present: Generated API route exports a default handler function.
6. test_route_name_in_generated_files: RECIPE_PARAM_ROUTE=users → generated files reference "users" in appropriate places (function name, route path).
7. test_no_template_literal_injection: RECIPE_PARAM_ROUTE contains spaces/special chars — generated files are syntactically valid and don't break.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-scaffold-templates.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-scaffold-templates.sh
- [ ] Valid bash syntax
  Verify: bash -n tests/scripts/test-scaffold-templates.sh
- [ ] Tests fail in RED phase
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-scaffold-templates.sh 2>&1; [ $? -ne 0 ]
